checks.finding: All AC-NNN IDs referenced in tasks.md Requirements fields (AC-001..AC-017, used across T-001..T-010) resolve to real criteria in acceptance-tests.md (AC-001 through AC-017 all present). No dangling IDs.

Every task's Blockers field is either the literal 'None' (T-001, T-006) or a comma-separated list of valid T-NNN IDs (e.g. T-002: 'T-001'; T-004: 'T-002, T-003, T-005'; T-010: 'T-002, T-003, T-004, T-005, T-006, T-007, T-008, T-009'). No range notation or prose found. Matches precheck's blockers_format_valid: true.

REQ-001 through REQ-011 (11 total) all appear in at least one task's Requirements field: REQ-001(T-001,T-004,T-010), REQ-002(T-001,T-002,T-004), REQ-003(T-002,T-003), REQ-004(T-005), REQ-005(T-006), REQ-006(T-007), REQ-007(T-008), REQ-008(T-007), REQ-009(T-001,T-010), REQ-010(T-009), REQ-011(T-002). No uncovered requirement.
AC-001 through AC-017 (17 total) all appear in at least one task's Requirements field, e.g. AC-005/AC-006/AC-014/AC-017 in T-005, AC-009/AC-015 in T-008, AC-012 in T-009. No uncovered AC.

All 10 tasks (T-001..T-010) cite at least one REQ-NNN in their Requirements field (e.g. T-005: 'REQ-004, AC-005, AC-006, AC-014, AC-017'; T-009: 'REQ-010, AC-012'). The 'Global Constraints' section preceding T-001 is project-wide context, not a task, and is correctly excluded from this check.

No task title contains 'test', 'spec', or 'verify' (case-insensitive) and no task's scope is exclusively test-file changes -- every task's Planned Files include substantive non-test artifacts (SKILL.md files, plugin.json manifests, agent definitions, scripts) alongside its test files. No orphaned test task exists to evaluate.

All 10 tasks declare 'Approval: Draft' and 'Status: Planned' (verified at lines 50/52, 124/126, 210/212, 276/278, 357/359, 461/463, 547/549, 638/640, 718/720, 785/787), matching the round-1 attempt-1 requirement.

All 10 tasks declare valid Risk/Required Workflow pairs matching the risk-gate-matrix: T-001 low/test-after, T-002 medium/acceptance-first, T-003 medium/acceptance-first, T-004 medium/acceptance-first, T-005 high/tdd, T-006 high/tdd, T-007 medium/acceptance-first, T-008 medium/acceptance-first, T-009 low/test-after, T-010 low/test-after. precheck-result.json confirms workflow_match_precheck: PASS; no mismatches found on independent verification.

AC-016 is claimed as a primary test target by both T-002 (Requirements: '...AC-016') and T-004 (Requirements: '...AC-016') with no differentiated verification in T-002. T-002's Done When list (lines 183-196) contains only artifact-set-generation and stage-resume verification items ('artifact-set.Tests.ps1 proves a fixture run produces all eight artifact paths...', 'A re-run against an already-checkpointed stage resumes rather than restarting from stage 1') -- no item verifies AC-016's actual subject matter (update-mode re-run of edited+downstream stages in confirmation mode, upstream byte-identical, status reset to Pending). T-004 is the sole owner of that verification via 'tests/sdd-domain/update-mode.Tests.ps1' (explicit Done-When item at line 341-344). T-002's citation of AC-016 in its Requirements field has no distinguishing scope or test evidence, unlike the acceptable AC-004 split (T-002 vs T-003, direct-seed-intake vs reverse-seed, each with its own differentiated Done-When test) or the acceptable AC-001/AC-011 splits (T-004/T-010 and T-001/T-010, skill-level vs repo-wide validate-repository verification, each explicit in Out-of-Scope text).

Every task ID referenced in a Blockers field (T-001 through T-009) exists in dependency-graph.json's nodes array (['T-001'..'T-010']) and in tasks.md itself. All 20 edges in dependency-graph.json reference only existing node IDs. No dangling blocker references.

Traced dependency-graph.json's 20 edges via DFS from every node; the graph is a DAG rooted at T-001/T-006 (no outgoing edges) with T-010 as the sole sink depending on all others. No path revisits a node (e.g. T-004 -> T-005 -> T-001 terminal; T-004 -> T-003 -> T-002 -> T-001 terminal). No cycle detected.

Task titles/scopes using 'and' were checked: T-001 ('scaffold and domain-contract schema') bundles two inert, foundational scaffolding artifacts with no independent behavior -- acceptable housekeeping bundle. T-004 ('mode routing and update mode') treats update as one of the routed modes, not a separate concern. T-005 ('domain-review-loop, domain-reviewer-a/b, and cross-model-verify wiring') is one coherent review/approval-gate pipeline stage. T-007 ('domain-sync and DOMAIN-CONFORMANCE reviewer checks') shares one trigger condition (approved domain/ model present/absent), one AC-010 absence-regression requirement, and combined Done-When verification -- a single downstream-consumption integration concern, not two distinct features. No task combines two independent feature concerns under 'and'.

All 10 tasks' Done When lists avoid forbidden vague verbs ('ensure', 'consider', 'verify is correct', 'works correctly', unqualified 'review'/'confirm'/'update'). Each task's boilerplate items ('traceability.md updated with T-NNN -> <exact REQ/AC list> mapping') name the exact file and exact content change. Every task additionally names a concrete verification artifact beyond the boilerplate: e.g. T-002 'tests/sdd-domain/artifact-set.Tests.ps1 proves a fixture run produces all eight artifact paths...and validates against the T-001 schema'; T-003 'Fixture investigation.md produces a non-empty candidate seed with at least one candidate context and one candidate term'; T-006 fixture list with explicit accept/reject outcomes; T-010 'tests/validate-repository.ps1 passes cleanly (7 plugins, 26 skills, 6 public skills, all manifests version-locked)'. No task lacks an inspectable, measurable completion criterion.

All 10 task IDs (T-001..T-010) appear in traceability.md's Code Target column. All 11 REQ IDs cited in traceability.md (REQ-001..REQ-011) exist in requirements.md. Full-profile layer-spec anchors verified against actual headings: REQ-001->ux-spec.md#scope-and-user-journeys (heading 'Scope and User Journeys' confirmed), REQ-002->ux-spec.md#interaction-sequence (confirmed), REQ-003->ux-spec.md#component-states (confirmed), REQ-005->security-spec.md#authorization (confirmed), REQ-007->security-spec.md#security-tests (confirmed), REQ-009->infra-spec.md#cicd-sequence (confirmed via 'CI/CD Sequence'), REQ-010->infra-spec.md#observability (confirmed), REQ-011->ux-spec.md#accessibility (confirmed); REQ-004/REQ-006/REQ-008 carry reasoned 'N/A -- cross-layer only: <reason>' values per the calibration's allowed alternative. No dangling references in either direction.
